verdict_reasoning: HSP60 is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin, but it has well-documented ecto-surface display on tumor cells and on stressed/apoptotic cells, where it acts as a danger signal engaged by innate immune receptors. This cancer/stress-induced outer-leaflet pool has been targeted by antibodies and is the basis for HSP60-directed immunotherapy concepts. Baseline localization is intramitochondrial, so surface accessibility is state-dependent.
